turns.user_message: I noticed yesterday that I completely forgot to look forward to the weekend, which is pretty strange for me. Usually, I get a little buzz when I know I have free time coming up, but this time it just felt like another block of hours. I tried to force myself to get excited about a movie I had been waiting for, but the feeling just never showed up. It is kind of annoying how my brain just skips past the anticipation part now. I keep waiting for that familiar spark to return, but everything just feels completely muted.

This flat feeling has been sticking around for a while now, and I am starting to realize it is not just a passing phase. Every single day feels exactly the same shade of gray, no matter what I try to do to shake it up. I used to care so much about little wins and simple pleasures, but now they just wash right over me without leaving a mark. It is exhausting to keep pretending that I am engaged when my mind is completely blank inside. I keep checking my own reactions like I am waiting for a delayed response that never actually arrives.

I am honestly starting to wonder if I will ever feel that spark again, because the emptiness has just swallowed everything up. I go through my days doing exactly what I am supposed to do, but there is absolutely nothing behind my eyes to match the actions. It feels like I am living in a completely soundproof room where nothing can actually reach me anymore. I try to remember what it was like to genuinely care about something, but the memory just slips away before I can hold onto it. Everything is just background noise now, and I am completely numb to it all.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
